paper_title: The antimicrobial activity and chemical composition of Elsholtzia blanda (Benth.) Benth. essential oils in Lam Dong Province, Viet Nam

entities.description: Researcher at Dalat University and primary author of the study.
Researcher at the Faculty of Biology, Dalat University.
Academic department where the research was conducted in Viet Nam.
The study of the chemical composition of plants.
The study of antimicrobial activities against bacteria and yeast.
The ability of the essential oil to inhibit the growth of microorganisms.
The identification of specific chemical constituents within the essential oil.
Process used to extract essential oils from the aerial parts of Elsholtzia blanda.
Analytical method used to identify and quantify chemical constituents.
Bioassay used to evaluate the antibacterial and antifungal activity.
Biological plant material used as the source for essential oil extraction.
Mass spectral database used for identification of chemical compounds.
Digital mass spectral library used for matching GC-MS data.
The percentage of oil obtained from the plant material.
The concentration of the most abundant constituent in the oil.
The concentration of the second most abundant constituent.
Total count of chemical constituents identified in the essential oil.
The threshold for considering mean differences as significant.
